observation.summary: Tool-use gate docs mixed implemented branches with reserved policy vocabulary, while executable evidence lived across policy, toolpolicy, toolargs, session, and supervisor projection code.

hypothesis.summary: A manifest can generate the reader doc, prove implemented tool-use branches, and keep allow-but-audit/quarantine reserved until new source evidence exists.

execution.summary: Run toolusegateevidence to check generated docs, source checks, absent reserved policy actions, and toolpolicy non-execution boundaries.

evaluation.summary: The verifier rejects generated doc drift, missing source evidence, unknown fact references, accidental reserved action implementation, and toolpolicy process execution leaks.

retrospective.summary: Tool-use security gate knowledge becomes executable evidence with explicit implemented/reserved branch separation.

evidence.proves: tool-use gate manifest matches generated docs, source evidence, and absent boundary rules
public CI uploads tool-use gate evidence
